name: Chinese Steamed Buns with Roast Pork (Cha Shao Pao)
recipeInstructions:
- For filling:
- 1 tablespoon peanut oil
- 1/2 pound cha Shao (Chinese Roasted Pork)
- 1 small leeks, thinly sliced
- 1 piece sliced ginger root
- For sauce:
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- 1 pinch salt
- 1/2 teaspoon monosodium glutamate (optional)
- 1 tablespoon soy sauce (dark)
- 1 teaspoon soy sauce (light)
- 1 tablespoon oyster sauce
- 1/2 cup chicken stock
- 1 teaspoon sesame oil
- 1 dash red food coloring
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch dissolved in water
Sift the flour into a large mixing bowl. Dissolve the sugar in hot water, add the yeast, and mix well; leave to stand for 10 minutes. Add the yeast to the flour and mix in the melted shortening or lard. Mix well, remove from the bowl, and knead for about 3 minutes. Shape into a long sausage shape and cover with a cloth.
Blend the sauce ingredients together and set aside. Heat the oil in a wok and fry the diced cha Shao, leek, and ginger over high heat for a minute. Remove the leek and ginger and discard; pour in the sauce and then add the cornstarch and water to thicken. Stir and cook until the mixture is smooth and homogeneous. Leave to cool.
Cut the roll of dough into 24 pieces, flattening each piece with your fingers and shaping into a disk. Place a tablespoon of filling in the center of each round of dough and enclose, pinching the dough closed with the fingers. Place a piece of waxed paper or foil under each dumpling and leave it to rise for 10 minutes.
Cook in a bamboo steamer for about 10 minutes, taking care to leave enough space between each dumpling to expand. Do not open the steamer while the dumplings are cooking. Serve warm.
